description: Governance driven by righteousness, identity, and social justice [1, 3].

sections.section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
key_promises: Implementation of a Caste Survey to ensure proportional representation [4].
Demanding a fair financial devolution from the Union Government, fighting for Tamil Nadu's rightful share up to the Supreme Court [4].
Securing a fair share of GST revenue by advocating for 70% to be allocated to states [4].
Taking legal and parliamentary steps to transfer Education back to the State List [4].
Strongly opposing constituency delimitation based on population to protect Southern states' representation [5].
Opposing the 'One Nation, One Election' proposal which weakens state autonomy [5].
Protecting the Two-Language Policy (Tamil and English) and rejecting the three-language formula [5].
Applying continuous pressure to abolish the NEET exam [5].
Advocating for state languages to be the official language of respective High Courts [5].
Establishing a Supreme Court branch in Chennai [5].
Securing Cauvery River water rights and ensuring proper monitoring [6].
Retrieving Katchatheevu fishing rights and preventing the arrest of TN fishermen by Sri Lanka [6].
Ensuring justice, citizenship rights, and access to state welfare schemes for Sri Lankan Tamils living in Tamil Nadu [6].
Taking action to abolish the Governor's post and transferring Vice-Chancellor appointment powers to the state government [6].
Allocating a ₹500 Crore financial budget to protect Tamil heritage and civilization [7].
Continuing archaeological excavations in 10 sites including Keeladi and Adichanallur [7].
Establishing an international standard 'Tamil Civilization Museum' in Madurai [7].
Implementing a Folk Artists Welfare Development scheme providing 100 days of work and a ₹15,000 aid for instruments [7].
Setting up Folk Art Research and Training Centers across various districts [8].
Promoting traditional sports through a 'Rural Olympics' and providing high compensation (₹10 Lakhs) for families of martyrs in Jallikattu [8].
Integrating the 'Thirukkural Development Scheme' into education with cash prizes for students [8].
Increasing the pension for Language Martyrs to ₹2,50,000 annually [9].
Developing Tamil Artificial Intelligence (AI) models and providing commercial rights to foreign countries free of charge [9].
Creating digital archives to protect Tamil arts, culture, and heritage [9].
Establishing a Tamil Nadu Digital Museum using AR, VR, and Hologram technologies [9].
Promoting 'Tamil Nadu Responsible Tourism' to share 25% of revenue for local livelihood development [10].
Developing Archaeological Tourism with evening folk events and cultural night markets [10].
Creating a Spiritual Tourism Scheme with dedicated circuits (Arupadaiveedu, Saiva, Vaishnava, and all-religion harmony circuits) [10].
Building Rest Houses for devotees at major spiritual sites [11].
Implementing an AR/VR-based Tamil Cultural Tourism Scheme at sites like Keeladi [11].
Providing tax concessions and 50% renovation subsidies for Tamil Cultural Lodges (heritage buildings like Chettinad houses) [11].
Establishing a Coastal Corridor Tourism from Vedaranyam to Rameswaram [11].
Promoting Nilgiris Mountain Adventure Tourism, including a Mountain Biking Festival [11].
Developing Caravan Tourism with ₹2 Lakh subsidies for entrepreneurs [12].
Creating a Food Tourism Corridor to showcase traditional foods globally [12].
Introducing Riverfront Tourism along the Cauvery and Thamirabarani rivers [12].
Implementing a Tourism Skill Development Scheme [12].
Promoting Cinema Tourism via QR codes at popular filming locations [12].
Ensuring Tourism Safety with better infrastructure and 24/7 help centers [12].

pillars.title: Dignified Life and Social Security for All [13]

key_promises: Creating a Poverty-Free Tamil Nadu by identifying poor families and guaranteeing jobs or self-employment [13].
Providing 200 units of Free Electricity monthly to eligible households [13].
Ensuring 100% clean drinking water supply to all households [13].
Providing ₹5 Lakh to poor rural people to build their own homes [13].
Regularizing Patta documents for those living on government land without objection [13].
Reforming the Public Distribution System (PDS) with doorstep delivery for vulnerable groups, fortified items, packaged goods to prevent adulteration, and establishing more ration shops [14].
Ensuring the Social Justice Budget allocated for marginalized communities is fully utilized for their development [15].
Providing an annual scholarship of ₹40,000 for SC/ST students in professional courses [15].
Offering an initial investment of ₹5 Lakh for first-generation SC/ST entrepreneurs [15].
Completely waiving off TAHDCO loans [15].
Providing permanent housing with Patta for all SC/ST people [15].
Establishing separate police divisions in every district to prevent atrocities against SC/ST communities [15].
Implementing the Sachar Committee Recommendations [16].
Ensuring minority participation in education and entrepreneurship, including opening colleges for minority women [16].
Protecting minority-run educational and charitable institutions [16].
Conducting a legal battle in the Supreme Court against the new Waqf Amendment Act [16].
Providing pilgrimage subsidies for Hajj, Jerusalem, and all religious sacred journeys [16].
Allocating adequate spaces for cemeteries and Kabarsthans [16].

sections.section_name: Senior Citizens and Differently Abled Welfare [17, 18]
key_promises: Appointing senior citizens as advisors in government administration [17].
Establishing an Elderly Protection Cell with a Nodal Officer in every SP office [17].
Providing doorstep government services like ration card renewals and certificates for senior citizens [17].
Increasing the monthly pension for differently-abled individuals to ₹3,000 [17].
Appointing special teachers for special children in every public school [17].
Providing financial assistance for assistive devices [17].
Strictly implementing the 4% reservation for differently-abled people in employment [19].
Introducing a Transgender Welfare and Dignity Protection Act with skill training and self-help groups [19].
Providing an annual Weaver Assistance Fund of ₹30,000 to those owning their own looms [19].
Offering a 50% procurement subsidy for weaving raw materials [21].
Increasing free electricity to 1500 units for powerlooms and 500 units for handlooms, plus up to 75% subsidy for solar panels [21].
Providing ₹10 Lakh life insurance for all weavers [21].
Creating Integrated Weaver Cities and an exclusive e-commerce brand ('Vettri Thari') [21].
Evaluating and implementing the Old Pension Scheme based on employee demands [18].
Creating a Government Employees Welfare Board with a ₹5000 Crore corpus [18].
Making temporary workers permanent after 5 years of service [18].
Ensuring transparency in promotions and transfers [18].
Immediate implementation of the 8th Pay Commission recommendations [18].
Ensuring "Equal Pay for Equal Work" for temporary teachers matching permanent teachers [20].
Sending outstanding government school teachers for training in Singapore and Finland [20].
Removing government school teachers from non-teaching and administrative tasks [23].
Increasing Anganwadi and ASHA worker salaries (₹18,000 and ₹10,000 respectively) and converting them to Grade-D employees [23].
Eradicating manual scavenging and providing mechanized training and ₹4,000 monthly pensions to retired sanitation workers [24].
Establishing an Auto-Taxi Driver Welfare Board with ₹10 Lakh accident/life insurance [24].
Launching a commission-free 'Tamil Taxi App' [25].
Enacting a Tamil Nadu Gig Workers Act for social protection and providing interest-free loans for e-bikes [22, 25].
Passing a Street Vendors Livelihood Protection Act offering ₹50,000 interest-free loans [22].
Reforming the Construction Workers Welfare Board and providing ₹5 Lakh accident insurance [26].
Setting up a ₹200 Crore corpus for Palm Tree Protection and offering ₹3,000 monthly aid during off-seasons for climbers [26].
